title: SB 1395 gives local governments the tools to fast track building interim housing and scale up efforts to bring people indoors and save lives
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California has the fourth highest rate of homelessness and the highest rate of unsheltered homelessness in the nation.",
    "California's homeless population reached a record 181,399 individuals in 2023.",
    "SB 1395 will encourage the development of interim housing by clarifying that relocatable, non-congregate interim housing is eligible for streamlined zoning.",
    "Streamlined zoning reduces construction time and costs for interim housing.",
    "SB 1395 cuts red tape and expedites approvals for local governments wanting to build interim housing.",
    "SB 1395 extends sunset for existing streamlining authorities beyond 2026.",
    "SB 1395 aims to free up state funding for interim housing.",
    "SB 1395 creates an expedited process to build more housing options.",
    "More interim housing can help unhoused individuals return to permanent housing.",
    "Interim housing can get people suffering from homelessness off the streets faster and at a fraction of the cost.",
    "SB 1395 will speed up development timelines to help end encampments.",
    "Interim housing is a key part of a comprehensive response to homelessness in California.",
    "Interim housing communities can be built in a matter of months.",
    "SB 1395 is expected to provide decent homes for people experiencing homelessness rapidly.",
    "Interim housing is proven, scalable, and serves as a near-term solution to provide stability and dignity to California’s unsheltered population."
  ]
}
```
